TASK: Audit and REMOVE unused dependencies from the Fauna & Flora store project.

CONTEXT: React SPA at /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store. Sprint 3 task S3-1.4. The package.json has 50+ dependencies including 20+ Radix UI packages, many of which may not be actually imported anywhere.

STEPS:
1. Read /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store/package.json to get the full dependency list
2. For EACH dependency, search all .ts/.tsx files in client/src/ and server/ for import statements referencing each package. Also check vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json, components.json.
3. For Radix UI packages: check if they're actually used by the shadcn/ui wrapper components in client/src/components/ui/. A Radix package is USED only if a UI component that imports it is itself imported somewhere in pages/ or components/ (not just existing in ui/).
4. Build a list of UNUSED packages. These commonly unused packages to look for:
   - Radix packages whose UI wrapper is never imported (e.g., hover-card, menubar, context-menu, radio-group, slider, progress, etc.)
   - react-resizable-panels, recharts, input-otp, cmdk, vaul, react-day-picker, embla-carousel-autoplay if unused
   - Dev deps that aren't referenced: add, @builder.io/vite-plugin-jsx-loc, vite-plugin-manus-runtime
5. Run: cd /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store && pnpm remove <space-separated-list-of-unused-packages>
6. Run: cd /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store && pnpm run check && pnpm run build
7. If build fails, add back the package that caused the failure and retry
8. Report: packages removed, bundle size before vs after, build status

IMPORTANT:
- Do NOT remove: react, react-dom, wouter, lucide-react, tailwind-merge, class-variance-authority, clsx, express, sonner, @radix-ui/react-slot, framer-motion, fuse.js, nanoid, react-helmet-async, compression, sharp
- Do NOT remove any devDependency that's used in build toolchain (vite, typescript, esbuild, tailwindcss, @tailwindcss/vite, @vitejs/plugin-react, autoprefixer, postcss, prettier, tsx, vitest)
- DO remove the ui/ wrapper files themselves if their Radix dep is removed
- Build MUST pass after removals

TASK: Add an add-to-cart confirmation toast notification to the Fauna & Flora store.

CONTEXT: React SPA at /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store. Sprint 3 task S3-2.5. The app already uses `sonner` for toasts (imported in App.tsx as `<Toaster />`). The cart logic is in client/src/contexts/CartContext.tsx. The `addToCart` function in CartContext does not currently show any feedback.

The ProductDetail page has an 'Add to Cart' button that calls `addToCart(product, quantity)`. The Shop page has product cards but they link to product detail pages.

STEPS:
1. Read /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store/client/src/contexts/CartContext.tsx
2. Read /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store/client/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx to see how addToCart is called
3. Modify the `addToCart` function in CartContext.tsx to trigger a sonner toast after adding the item:
   - Import `toast` from 'sonner' at the top of CartContext.tsx
   - After the setItems call in addToCart, call:
     ```
     toast(`${product.name} added to cart`, {
       description: `Quantity: ${quantity}`,
       action: {
         label: 'View Cart',
         onClick: () => window.location.href = '/cart',
       },
       duration: 3000,
     });
     ```
4. Run: cd /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store && pnpm run check && pnpm run build
5. Report: what was changed, build status

IMPORTANT:
- Use sonner's `toast()` function, NOT react-hot-toast or any other library
- The toast should auto-dismiss after 3 seconds
- Include a 'View Cart' action button in the toast
- The toast should show the product name
- Do NOT modify the Toaster component props in App.tsx
- Keep it minimal - no thumbnail image in the toast (sonner doesn't support that easily)
- Build must pass

TASK: Improve the newsletter CTA value proposition in the Fauna & Flora store footer. CONTEXT: React SPA at /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store. Sprint 3 task S3-4.1. The current newsletter section in the footer (Layout.tsx) has: - Title: "Join the Ritual" - Description: "Receive grooming tips and early access to new batches." This is generic and doesn't give a compelling reason to subscribe. STEPS: 1. Read /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store/client/src/components/Layout.tsx 2. Find the newsletter section in the footer (it's the last column in the grid, with heading "Join the Ritual") 3. Update the copy to be more compelling: - Keep the heading "Join the Ritual" (it fits the brand) - Change the description to: "Get 10% off your first order, ancestral skincare tips, and early access to new small-batch releases. Monthly wisdom, never spam." - This adds: a concrete incentive (10% off), specific value (ancestral skincare tips), frequency expectation (monthly), and anti-spam assurance 4. Do NOT change the NewsletterForm component, the form functionality, or the privacy policy link 5. Do NOT change any styling or layout 6. Run: cd /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store && pnpm run check && pnpm run build 7. Report: exact text changed, build status IMPORTANT: - Only change the

tag text between the h4 heading and the component - Keep the consent/privacy text below the form as-is - Build must pass

TASK: Add Sentry error monitoring to the Fauna & Flora store (client-side only for now).

CONTEXT: React SPA at /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store. Sprint 3 task S3-5.1. No error monitoring currently exists. The app uses React 19, Vite 7, and is deployed as a static SPA with an Express server.

STEPS:
1. Install Sentry: cd /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store && pnpm add @sentry/react
2. Read /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store/client/src/main.tsx
3. Add Sentry initialization in main.tsx BEFORE ReactDOM.createRoot:
   ```typescript
   import * as Sentry from '@sentry/react';
   
   if (import.meta.env.VITE_SENTRY_DSN) {
     Sentry.init({
       dsn: import.meta.env.VITE_SENTRY_DSN,
       environment: import.meta.env.MODE,
       integrations: [
         Sentry.browserTracingIntegration(),
         Sentry.replayIntegration({ maskAllText: false, blockAllMedia: false }),
       ],
       tracesSampleRate: 0.1,
       replaysSessionSampleRate: 0.1,
       replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0,
     });
   }
   ```
4. Read /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store/client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx
5. Wrap the ErrorBoundary component with Sentry.withErrorBoundary or integrate Sentry.captureException in the componentDidCatch:
   - In the catch handler, add: `Sentry.captureException(error, { extra: { componentStack: errorInfo?.componentStack } });`
   - Import Sentry at the top of ErrorBoundary.tsx
6. Add Vite source map upload plugin:
   - pnpm add -D @sentry/vite-plugin
   - Read /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store/vite.config.ts
   - Add the sentryVitePlugin to the plugins array (conditionally, only when SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN env is set):
     ```typescript
     import { sentryVitePlugin } from '@sentry/vite-plugin';
     // In plugins array, at the end:
     ...(process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN ? [sentryVitePlugin({
       org: process.env.SENTRY_ORG || 'fauna-flora',
       project: process.env.SENTRY_PROJECT || 'fauna-flora-store',
       authToken: process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN,
     })] : []),
     ```
   - Also add `build: { sourcemap: true }` if not already present (merge with existing build config)
7. Run: cd /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store && pnpm run check && pnpm run build
8. Report: files modified, build status, instructions for user to set VITE_SENTRY_DSN

IMPORTANT:
- Sentry init is gated behind VITE_SENTRY_DSN env var - it does nothing if not set
- Source map plugin is gated behind SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN - build works fine without it
- Do NOT add sentry to the server/index.ts (server-side monitoring deferred)
- tracesSampleRate 0.1 = 10% of transactions (cost-conscious)
- Build MUST pass even without Sentry DSN configured

TASK: Set up uptime monitoring for the Fauna & Flora store using a Zo scheduled agent.

CONTEXT: Sprint 3 task S3-5.3. The production site is at https://faunaflorabotanicals.com. We need to monitor homepage, /shop, /api/csrf-token (as a health check endpoint), and /sitemap.xml. Since we can't install external services easily, we'll create a monitoring script + Zo agent.

STEPS:
1. Create a monitoring script at /home/workspace/Scripts/ffb-uptime-check.sh:
   ```bash
   #!/usr/bin/env bash
   set -euo pipefail
   
   ENDPOINTS=(
     "https://faunaflorabotanicals.com|Homepage"
     "https://faunaflorabotanicals.com/shop|Shop"
     "https://faunaflorabotanicals.com/api/csrf-token|API Health"
     "https://faunaflorabotanicals.com/sitemap.xml|Sitemap"
   )
   
   FAILURES=""
   RESULTS=""
   
   for entry in "${ENDPOINTS[@]}"; do
     url="${entry%%|*}"
     name="${entry##*|}"
     
     status=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 30 "$url" || echo "000")
     time=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{time_total}' --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 30 "$url" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
     
     if [ "$status" -ge 200 ] && [ "$status" -lt 400 ]; then
       RESULTS="$RESULTS\n OK | $name | ${status} | ${time}s"
     else
       RESULTS="$RESULTS\n FAIL | $name | ${status} | ${time}s"
       FAILURES="$FAILURES $name($status)"
     fi
   done
   
   echo -e "FFB Uptime Check - $(date -u +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC')\n$RESULTS"
   
   if [ -n "$FAILURES" ]; then
     echo "\nFAILURES DETECTED:$FAILURES"
     exit 1
   fi
   
   echo "\nAll endpoints healthy."
   ```
2. Make it executable: chmod +x /home/workspace/Scripts/ffb-uptime-check.sh
3. Test the script: bash /home/workspace/Scripts/ffb-uptime-check.sh
4. Report: script output showing all endpoints checked, their status codes and response times

IMPORTANT:
- The script should exit 0 on all-healthy, exit 1 on any failure
- Use curl with connect-timeout and max-time to avoid hangs
- Do NOT create a Zo agent (the user will configure alerts separately)
- Just create and test the monitoring script

TASK: Create an automated weekly security header audit script for the Fauna & Flora store.

CONTEXT: Sprint 3 task S3-5.4. The site is at https://faunaflorabotanicals.com. The server already sets security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, X-XSS-Protection, Permissions-Policy) in server/index.ts.

STEPS:
1. Create a script at /home/workspace/Scripts/ffb-security-headers-audit.sh:
   ```bash
   #!/usr/bin/env bash
   set -euo pipefail
   
   URL="https://faunaflorabotanicals.com"
   REPORT_DIR="/home/workspace/Reports/security-headers"
   mkdir -p "$REPORT_DIR"
   DATE=$(date -u +'%Y-%m-%d')
   REPORT="$REPORT_DIR/audit-$DATE.md"
   
   echo "# Security Headers Audit - $DATE" > "$REPORT"
   echo "" >> "$REPORT"
   echo "**URL:** $URL" >> "$REPORT"
   echo "" >> "$REPORT"
   
   HEADERS=$(curl -sI --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 30 "$URL")
   
   REQUIRED_HEADERS=(
     "strict-transport-security"
     "content-security-policy"
     "x-frame-options"
     "x-content-type-options"
     "referrer-policy"
     "x-xss-protection"
     "permissions-policy"
   )
   
   PASS=0
   FAIL=0
   
   echo "| Header | Status | Value |" >> "$REPORT"
   echo "|--------|--------|-------|" >> "$REPORT"
   
   for header in "${REQUIRED_HEADERS[@]}"; do
     value=$(echo "$HEADERS" | grep -i "^$header:" | head -1 | sed 's/^[^:]*: //' | tr -d '\r')
     if [ -n "$value" ]; then
       echo "| $header | PASS | $value |" >> "$REPORT"
       ((PASS++))
     else
       echo "| $header | **MISSING** | - |" >> "$REPORT"
       ((FAIL++))
     fi
   done
   
   echo "" >> "$REPORT"
   echo "## Summary" >> "$REPORT"
   echo "- **Passed:** $PASS/${#REQUIRED_HEADERS[@]}" >> "$REPORT"
   echo "- **Missing:** $FAIL/${#REQUIRED_HEADERS[@]}" >> "$REPORT"
   
   # Check for x-powered-by (should be absent)
   if echo "$HEADERS" | grep -qi "^x-powered-by:"; then
     echo "- **WARNING:** X-Powered-By header is exposed (should be removed)" >> "$REPORT"
   else
     echo "- X-Powered-By correctly hidden" >> "$REPORT"
   fi
   
   echo "" >> "$REPORT"
   echo "Report saved to: $REPORT"
   cat "$REPORT"
   
   if [ $FAIL -gt 0 ]; then
     exit 1
   fi
   ```
2. Make it executable: chmod +x /home/workspace/Scripts/ffb-security-headers-audit.sh
3. Test the script: bash /home/workspace/Scripts/ffb-security-headers-audit.sh
4. Report: full audit results

IMPORTANT:
- The script should output a markdown report AND save it to Reports/security-headers/
- Exit 0 if all headers present, exit 1 if any missing
- Do NOT create a Zo agent for scheduling (user will configure separately)
- Just create and test the script

TASK: Run the final build and deploy the Fauna & Flora store with all Sprint 3 changes.

CONTEXT: React SPA at /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store. All Sprint 3 code changes should now be applied. Need to: verify TypeScript, build, and restart the production service.

STEPS:
1. cd /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store
2. Run TypeScript check: pnpm run check
   - If errors, fix them (read the error messages, find the files, fix the issues)
   - Re-run check until 0 errors
3. Run production build: pnpm run build
   - If errors, fix them
   - Re-run build until success
4. List the dist/ output to verify:
   - ls -la dist/
   - ls -la dist/public/
   - Report total dist size
5. After successful build, deploy by restarting the Zo service:
   - Create a file at /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store/.deploy-marker with content: timestamp and 'Sprint 3 deployment'
   - The service (svc_p0qfXv2iDcI) runs `node dist/index.js` from /home/workspace/fauna-flora-store
   - The service will pick up the new dist/ on next restart
6. Verify the production site is accessible:
   - curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://faunaflorabotanicals.com
   - curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://faunaflorabotanicals.com/shop
7. Report:
   - TypeScript check: PASS/FAIL
   - Build: PASS/FAIL
   - Bundle sizes (main JS chunk, CSS)
   - Deployment status
   - Any warnings

IMPORTANT:
- Fix ALL TypeScript errors before building
- Fix ALL build errors
- Do NOT modify business logic - only fix compilation issues
- If a Sprint 3 change introduced a type error, fix it minimally
- Report the final dist/ sizes for comparison with previous sprints
